title: Eva At Work
wikitext:
[]: Eva At Work             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 01, 2006 11:33 am Some of you might have seen the rather peculiar Eva stuff spotlighted on Gainax's site of the past months. I can only describe it as a sort of cross-promotional campaign for commercial designers to utilize the substance of Eva to show what they're capable of. Weird, interesting, and cool, just a few words that would describe the whole thing, you can either visit the official Eva At Work page on Gainax's site, or you can take a look at the works that I have archived here on Eva Monkey. Enjoy, discuss, freak out. http://www.gainax.co.jp/eaw/index.html http://www.evamonkey.com/evaatwork/

[]:             Quote Post by Cogboy » Sat Jul 01, 2006 1:35 pm I'd seen the lilith dinner set before, and i really want to try some Pen Pen bento, but when i saw EVA 01 spreading that guys buttcheeks with its hands I just burst out laughing.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sat Jul 01, 2006 2:01 pm I WANT TO EAT THAT Rei!! i found everything freakin cool. I had seen some of them in THAT-BOARD.com i cannot understand the point of the last one, translation?

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Sat Jul 01, 2006 8:48 pm Cool stuff....

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Wed Jul 05, 2006 11:37 am Those are incredibly cool. The tattoo one is especially mental.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Fri Jul 21, 2006 6:19 am Edible EVA-01, ftw. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jul 22, 2006 11:37 am Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Edible EVA-01, ftw. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sat Jul 22, 2006 10:30 pm That Lilith plate is cool! I want that seven eyes to look at me while I have my dinner!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 28, 2006 10:40 am http://www.evamonkey.com/evaatwork/eaw_07.swf New Eva at Work. It's a wedding dress. Weirdness.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Jul 28, 2006 11:11 am For a wedding dress it remarkably looks like it was made out of a table cloth. I don't think it looks well made enough that I could even joke to my girl about wearing it to our wedding.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 28, 2006 12:42 pm I don't care if the bride and groom are both Eva fans, a wedding dress with Eva on it is friggin tacky as hell.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Jul 29, 2006 6:50 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't care if the bride and groom are both Eva fans, a wedding dress with Eva on it is friggin tacky as hell.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Sat Jul 29, 2006 7:50 am Haha, you'd have to be a real loser to get that dress for your GF. I love NGE, but not to that extent.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Aug 25, 2006 1:17 pm There's a new Eva at Work up. Main Page - http://www.gainax.co.jp/ Direct Link - http://www.gainax.co.jp/img/eaw_08.swf

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Sep 23, 2006 6:34 pm New Eva at Work. looks like a stained glass lamp type thing. Main Page - http://www.gainax.co.jp/ Direct Link - http://www.gainax.co.jp/img/eaw_09.swf

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Sep 24, 2006 6:57 am This is a good example of what happens when you mix Tiffany's and Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 30, 2006 12:24 am Main Page - http://www.gainax.co.jp/ Direct Link - http://www.gainax.co.jp/img/eaw_10.swf I don't get it, I assume it's the artist's abstract representation of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Oct 30, 2006 3:05 am I'm guessing that it's Tokyo-3

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Mon Oct 30, 2006 5:09 am Vaguely reminds me of the satanic figure from the opening.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Oct 30, 2006 7:36 am Instrumentality.

Article: Eva At Work
[]: Eva At Work             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 01, 2006 11:33 am Some of you might have seen the rather peculiar Eva stuff spotlighted on Gainax's site of the past months. I can only describe it as a sort of cross-promotional campaign for commercial designers to utilize the substance of Eva to show what they're capable of. Weird, interesting, and cool, just a few words that would describe the whole thing, you can either visit the official Eva At Work page on Gainax's site, or you can take a look at the works that I have archived here on Eva Monkey. Enjoy, discuss, freak out. http://www.gainax.co.jp/eaw/index.html http://www.evamonkey.com/evaatwork/
